rationale: Obligation unprovable from postconditions. DelegationMap.view() and DelegationMap.valid() are both #[verifier::external_body] pub closed spec fn (completely uninterpreted). Postconditions r1.valid()/r2.valid() and r1@==M, r2@==M yield only r1@==r2@ (provable, asserted via =~=). Nothing constrains the concrete fields lows: StrictlyOrderedMap<K> and m: Ghost<Map<...>>, and view is not injective, so structural equality r1==r2 cannot be derived without an axiom that the sandbox forbids (assume/external_body/admit). Analogous sibling case delegation_map_v__impl4__new__new previously failed for the same family of reasons (Vec/struct opacity). Proof block left documents the impossibility and propagates the strongest honest consequence.
